comment: Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1351335225|1351335225]] by [[Special:Contributions/Gingeroscar|Gingeroscar]] ([[User talk:Gingeroscar|talk]]) Changing the wording here requires so much more than the first half of the sentence. It says it flows towards another body of water, which implies it is already water. You could very well change wording, but do so in more than half a sentence.
Rivers exist on other planets. Titan's rivers are made of hydrocarbons. I made it the case that this article clearly specifies Terran rivers when it presumes fresh water.
[[Wikipedia:OABOT|Open access bot]]: url-access=subscription updated in citation with #oabot.
/* Terminus */ add info and citation per edit request
Changes made as per request on talk page by [[User:RustyHydrologist|RustyHydrologist]]
/* Movement of organisms */ Fixed some of the writing, and linked to the fungal group in question.
/* River Continuum Concept */ Added the Vannote et al. citation to the section writing about their proposed concept.
Rescuing 17 sources and tagging 0 as dead.) #IABot (v2.0.9.5
Add: article-number, doi. | [[:en:WP:UCB|Use this bot]]. [[:en:WP:DBUG|Report bugs]]. | Suggested by Abductive | #UCB_toolbar
Maybe the images could be upscaled a bit. That would aid in identification.
The subject of the image cannot be identified without zooming. [[MOS:IMG]] discourages arbitrarily sized images.
Changed /* Politics of rivers */ to /* Politics */ because its less redundant, as mentioned in [[MOS:SECTIONSTYLE]].

Removed /* Rivers by amount of discharge */. Such information has [[List of rivers by discharge|its own list]], and is listed in [[Lists of rivers]] linked in the see also section. Nothing here worthy of its own gallery on the main topic.
[[Wikipedia:OABOT|Open access bot]]: url-access=subscription updated in citation with #oabot.
Change the title of the section /* The terminus */ to /* Terminus */. Rephrased the section's image caption and one of its sentences.
Changed the section title /* The flow of rivers */ to /* Flow */ to comply with [[MOS:NOBACKREF]].
/* Definition */ Removed an image. Its content has nothing to do with this section.
rv recent deleterious edits to the lede

Restored revision 1291773016 by [[Special:Contributions/Randy Kryn|Randy Kryn]] ([[User talk:Randy Kryn|talk]]): It's not helpful to graft new material directly into the article lead—moreover, I don't see this factoid as anything much more, especially in context.
[[Wikipedia:OABOT|Open access bot]]: url-access=subscription updated in citation with #oabot.

Minor anecdote and grammar changes surrounding, not an attribution for that whole text. Idk what the styling is for that but I'm assuming it's overwhelming body of scholarship supports this assertion vs. relatively unknown information type referencing
/* See also */ added 'List of waterfalls' (pertains directly to rivers), spacing fix
[[Wikipedia:OABOT|Open access bot]]: url-access updated in citation with #oabot.
Reverted [[WP:AGF|good faith]] edits by [[Special:Contributions/Saddam19|Saddam19]] ([[User talk:Saddam19|talk]]): That image is not obviously related to the section.
[[Wikipedia:OABOT|Open access bot]]: doi updated in citation with #oabot.

Alter: journal, issue, pages. Add: encyclopedia, date, work, pmid, bibcode, authors 1-1. Removed parameters. Formatted [[WP:ENDASH|dashes]]. Some additions/deletions were parameter name changes. | [[:en:WP:UCB|Use this bot]]. [[:en:WP:DBUG|Report bugs]]. | Suggested by Abductive | [[Category:Rivers]] | #UCB_Category 44/98
Fixed references.  Please see [[Category:CS1 errors: dates]] and [[Category:CS1 errors: extra text: edition]].
Fixed reference date error(s) (see [[:Category:CS1 errors: dates|CS1 errors: dates]] for details) and [[WP:AWB/GF|AWB general fixes]]
Changed some wording in introduction, added some references (nothing at all for first couple of paragraphs), added some ecologies, and lots of hyperlinks.
/* Movement of organisms */ changed some wording to include more groups, and added a sentence about fungal movement in streams.

Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1285858078|1285858078]] by [[Special:Contributions/Smartiejl|Smartiejl]] ([[User talk:Smartiejl|talk]]) most of those are plain English terms; see [[WP:OVERLINK]]—we don't link every conceivable term
Changed a wikilink target to avoid a redirect.
Rephrased the first sentence because [[MOS:SOB|wikilinks should not be placed next to each other when possible]]. Its flow is not disrupted and there is no repetition.
Restored revision 1272813631 by [[Special:Contributions/LukeWiller|LukeWiller]] ([[User talk:LukeWiller|talk]]): Back to the non-fixated version
/* See also */ + link to the methane river on Titan ([[Vid Flumina]])
Rivers don't have to be water. Rivers are common on Titan and they are made of ethane and methane.
American English, [[Wikipedia:MOSNUMscript|script]]-assisted date audit and style fixes per [[MOS:NUM]]
/* The flow of rivers */ Removed parameters.
/* Politics of rivers */ Makes more sense to swap sentences around as the Lamari River isn't a national boundary
/* Politics of rivers */ Per the source, more accurate description. Slightly messes up the current DYK, but better to be correct.
rm period; it's not a complete sentence, per [[MOS:CAPFRAG]]
cleanup external links for mos concerns
/* Rivers by discharge */ Added word "ten" to gallery to limit it's scope a bit.
moved image based on talk page.
/* Topography */ moved image to the left.
/* Non-perennial rivers */ Added photo of Dried out rivers on Mars. Added citations to source stating non-perennial rivers may have once flowed on Mars.

Added section for rivers by discharge. Links to the main page for list. Contains a gallery with links to pages for the top 10 rivers that aren't tributaries of other rivers. Used lead image for each river for the gallery image. This addresses some talk page discussion on including images of rivers from diverse geographic areas. Please discuss changes on talk page, such as switching what the criteria for gallery section should be.
v2.05b - [[User:WikiCleanerBot#T18|Bot T18 CW#553]] - Fix errors for [[WP:WCW|CW project]] (<nowiki> tags)
/* See also */ Unnecessary, probably inappropriate small text, [[MOS:PSEUDOHEAD]]
Filled in 0 bare reference(s) with reFill 2
fixed [[MOS:DASH|dashes]] using [[User:Ohconfucius/dashes.js]], [[Wikipedia:MOSNUMscript|script]]-assisted date audit and style fixes per [[MOS:NUM]]
/* The flow of rivers */ another duped source
Changing [[Wikipedia:Short description|short description]] from "Natural flowing watercourse" to "Natural flowing freshwater stream"
more work organizing the tail content of the article

Alter: url, pages, title, chapter, template type. URLs might have been anonymized. Add: magazine, chapter, chapter-url, jstor, bibcode, doi, issue, authors 1-1. Removed or converted URL. Removed parameters. Formatted [[WP:ENDASH|dashes]]. Some additions/deletions were parameter name changes. | [[:en:WP:UCB|Use this bot]]. [[:en:WP:DBUG|Report bugs]]. | Suggested by ForksForks | #UCB_webform
Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1237900796|1237900796]] by [[Special:Contributions/ForksForks|ForksForks]] ([[User talk:ForksForks|talk]]) apparently this doesn't work.
ran reFill on the page to fix refs
new 'other uses' hatnote, cleanup a ref
rewrite lead mos complaint. kept images minus one that didn't fit.
distribute remaining content in streamflow section to other sections. some of this had already been distributed already but just needed cutting.
/* See also */ further refine see also section

/* Types and ratings */ remove two sections that are now duplicated in the 'topography' section (eg, subsurface rivers) and remove the language lesson section. Rewrite the explainer for stream order and wikilink the correct article (I think the see also: link that was there originally was misplaced possibly)
/* See also */ reinstate article that was mentioned as 'river chemistry' in see also section
in depth look at the ecology of rivers, replacing unsourced content. moved the fish zonation section to the rewritten ecology section.

Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1237841771|1237841771]] by [[Special:Contributions/Knowledge and philosophy|Knowledge and philosophy]] ([[User talk:Knowledge and philosophy|talk]]) We have a few too many images in this article already. And an image for such a short section (that doesn't appear to match the section's content) doesn't improve the article.
/* See also */ cut down see also section to not duplicate content of article
/* Uses */ move and rewrite unsourced 'management' section as a subsection for "uses". Also replaces some content under uses that was primarily unsourced.
/* Uses */ new section -- history of humans and rivers. this should hopefully be not controversial since there was previously next to no content on this subject.
/* Religion and mythology */ rewrite unsourced section on rivers as borders, etc.
expanded section for religion and mythology. includes more context on floods, the reasons behind river related myths, and more examples. preserves existing format.
rewrite 'threats' section, previous section was a stub and had a maintenance template calling for expansion
/* Topography */ include info about river deltas, as well as tidal rivers.
/* Topography */ add info about other kinds of rivers, including those that run underground and don't last all year round. since this content is in the lead we need to verify it.
/* References */ [[WP:LDR|list-defined references]] not being used, clean up [[CAT:REF|pages with incorrect ref formatting]]
/* Source and drainage basin */ publish the drainage basins image
/* Channels */ rewrite channels section, sourced to harcourt and francis, USGS, "all about watersheds". Previous section was mostly unsourced.
relocate existing images to redone section
/* Topography */ rewrite of source and drainage basin section, sourced mostly to USGS articles. previous section had no sourcing.

/* Topography */ add definitions for river related terms in a short subsection. this provides sources for much of the content in the lead that is not repeated anywhere in the article, per mos this is required.
Rescuing orphaned refs ("Rosenberg2006" from rev 1226031816; "Rosenberg1" from rev 1226031816)
restore previous long established version. Please discuss on talk page and seek consensus for such a major change
uppercase per proper name and Wikipedia style (Earth)
Undid revision 1188074569 by [[Special:Contributions/Fgnievinski|Fgnievinski]] ([[User talk:Fgnievinski|talk]]): need a better source
/* top */ Replaced mankind with humankind to use more gender-neutral language
Rescuing 1 sources and tagging 0 as dead.) #IABot (v2.0.9.3
/* Source and drainage basin */ moving terms into [[Current (hydrology)]]
Rescuing 4 sources and tagging 0 as dead.) #IABot (v2.0.9.3) ([[User:Referencer12|Referencer12]] - 12331
Fixed the cite errors: commented out two references left unused after their associated text was deleted by [[Special:Diff/1095750028]] in June.
use portal box with border for consistency
some text copied from the [[List of Common Misconceptions]] page, also linked to that page
/* Sacred rivers */ Fixed grammatical errors and made the section easier to follow.
v2.04b - [[User:WikiCleanerBot#T20|Bot T20 CW#61]] - Fix errors for [[WP:WCW|CW project]] (Reference before punctuation)
Ref 4 improved by mention of John Speed's atlas of 1611
Note added that older towns & cities are commonly located at the few locations where larger rivers could be crossed.
Replace or disable a template per [[Wikipedia:Templates for discussion/Log/2022 February 12|TFD outcome]]; no change in content
Cleanup and [[WP:AWB/T|typo fixing]], [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: east-west → east–west, present day → present-day
/* See also */ Added portal link(s) and portal link layout using [[Template:Portal-mobile]], so the link is viewable to mobile users. The boxed version does not show up in mobile view.
